Kalenjins and Kikuyus have been locked in an invisible political dance for decades because they needed each other. Moi understood this and kept Kikuyu deputies and allies close for much of his presidency. Ruto gambled that he could rule without the Kikuyu political establishment, and that gamble has damaged the relationship. The key difference is that Kikuyus has more coalition options. They can pivot to a Luhya alliance, while Kalenjin can't,they can only depend on Kikuyus for power. If the Kikuyu-Luhya partnership holds, the Kikuyus will back a Luhya presidential candidate first, then build an arrangement where the presidency alternates between the two blocs over time. Ruto's strategy has cost the Kalenjin political establishment and they will go looong before returning to the national cake.
